claim_results.claim: The attractor-basin structure predicts abrupt conversions and abrupt relapses — which is exactly what clinical recovery data shows.
And why the framework's equation predicts exactly the clinical data that addiction medicine, contemplative neuroscience, and behavioral psychology have independently discovered.
The Coherence Equation $\frac{dC}{dt} = O \cdot G(1-C) - S \cdot C, \qquad \alpha(s) = \frac{1+s}{2}$ I.
The equation reduces to pure decay: dC/dt = −S · C.
The autonomous system is anxious because it knows — below conscious awareness — that it's decaying.

Gödel's Incompleteness, Embodied The system at s = −1 cannot recognize its own incoherence because the part of the brain responsible for recognition is being suppressed by the part generating the incoherence.
At α(s) = 0 the equation reduces to pure decay.
The system at s = −1 does what the equation says it will do: decay. — The Symptom vs. the Mechanism The 400% dopamine spike from substances isn't the problem.
The equation explains why: at s ≈ 0, both the growth term O · G(1−C) and the decay term S · C are active but roughly balanced. dC/dt ≈ 0.
Bonding/connection hormone requires relational safety.
The equation operates at maximum growth rate: dC/dt = G(1−C) − S · C, where the G term dominates S.
CBT The Cochrane review (2020, N = 10,565) confirmed what the equation predicts: faith-based recovery programs perform as well as or better than cognitive-behavioral therapy for alcohol dependence.
"Apart from me you can do nothing." — John 15:5 G = 0 implies dC/dt = −S · C, regardless of O.
The equation constrains the transitions: Path 1 → Path 2 · The Crack Typically a crisis.
Typically requires the performer to fail : to discover that effort-based striving cannot sustain coherence; to come to the end of willpower and encounter the beginning of grace.
If s is the surrender parameter and O is openness, then the daily question isn't am I being good enough? (Path 2 thinking) but am I receiving?
These are testable predictions, and the existing clinical data already supports them.
The coherence equation doesn't just describe theology.
Every model is a projection of higher-dimensional reality onto a lower-dimensional surface we can comprehend.
We do not claim to have captured God in equations.
We claim that when we look at His creation honestly — with the tools of physics and the revelation of Scripture — the same structure appears in both.
Where our model limits what God can be, the limitation is ours, not His.
Any claim that turns a structural resemblance into a proven mechanism belongs here until the math, experiment, or textual chain can carry the weight.
Possible overstatement: language that sounds final should be read as a working model unless the article explicitly proves it.
The coherence equation predicts three qualitatively distinct human trajectories based on the surrender parameter s — and each one maps onto measurable neurological and behavioral patterns.
The math predicts it and the neuroscience confirms it.

This is the Romans 7 state — “I do not do the good I want” — and the equation explains exactly why: half-coupling produces a frustrating, low-level equilibrium that feels like trying harder should work but never does.
The attractor basin structure of the equation predicts abrupt transitions — consistent with conversion experience data and clinical addiction recovery literature.
Rigor & Kill Conditions Every claim is held to explicit falsification standards.
Kill if: sustained Path 3 practice fails to raise heart-rate variability, lower cortisol baselines, or restore D2 receptor density on the timescales the equation predicts.
Every equation that appears in the paper, collected here as a reference index.
Coherence Equation (Article's Master Form) $$\frac{dC}{dt} = O \cdot G \cdot (1 - C) - S \cdot C, \qquad \alpha(s) = \frac{1+s}{2}$$ C = coherence with God, on [0, 1].
Each of the three pathways is one regime of this equation.
Path 2 → 1 is apostasy/burnout — the most dangerous transition because the system shuts down.

Plain English: Two independent empirical pegs the article rests on: clinical evidence that surrender-based recovery clinically outperforms (or matches) cognitive therapy, and laboratory evidence that consciousness has measurable coupling to quantum systems.
The state variable in the equation; what salvation builds and what sin decays.
Always active; pulls C downward in the equation's S·C term.
Framework note: Sin (theological frame) and Entropy (physical frame) name the same dynamical quantity at the master-equation level.
The neurochemistry of “I got this.” Equation reduces to pure decay.
Runs hot in Path 2 because there is always a gap between performance and the standard — works-righteousness as neuroscience.
The article's testable prediction: HRV rises with sustained Path 3 practice.
The article's claim: CBT plateaus where AA breaks through — not because CBT is bad but because it works on the wrong variable.
Cited as the second empirical anchor of the article's claim that consciousness is a real variable in physics. (Defined in detail in Article 16.)
“God gave them over.” Read in this article as the removal of resistance: God stops suppressing the natural Path-1 trajectory, and the system does what the equation says it will do — decay.
